items.description: Large irregular loss of parchment at the bottom right corner. The corner is torn away, removing the final 3–4 lines of text on the right side of the column. Remaining edge is jagged, frayed, and abraded with visible fiber loss.
items.scholarly_note: Classic lower-outer corner wear from handling in a bound codex; produces a textual lacuna at the end of the section.

items.description: Highly irregular, ragged top edge with extensive material loss across the entire width. A distinct lighter horizontal band runs across the uppermost portion, consistent with prior adhesive residue or mounting damage.
items.scholarly_note: Suggests the folio suffered trimming, rebinding stress, or early conservation intervention; common in the Codex Regius transmission history.

items.region: right side, mid to upper text area
items.description: Large, dark brown to black stain extending from the right margin well into the text block. Several words are smudged or rendered partially illegible; ink has bled in the affected zone.
items.scholarly_note: Most likely water or liquid damage; the parchment here is thinner and more translucent than surrounding areas.

items.region: scattered, especially lower left, center, and margins
items.description: Multiple medium and small brown age-related stains and foxing spots distributed across the parchment surface, some overlapping text lines.
items.scholarly_note: Typical 13th-century vellum degradation from humidity fluctuations and organic residues; does not appear to postdate the original inscription.

items.description: Broad zone of heavy overall darkening and mottled discoloration across the upper third, overlaying the opening lines and reducing contrast.
items.scholarly_note: May reflect cumulative moisture exposure, smoke, or general environmental soiling over centuries.

items.description: Several faint linear scratches and creases running vertically and diagonally across multiple lines of text. Some scratches lift the parchment surface and interrupt the ink strokes.
items.scholarly_note: Post-inscription damage; the lines cross the written surface rather than guiding it.

items.description: Fine vertical and near-vertical abrasion scratches along the right margin, with minor surface fiber loss and slight edge rounding.
items.scholarly_note: Consistent with friction from binding or shelving contact.

scratches: Vertical and diagonal linear scratches crossing the lower text block, visible as light lines that break ink strokes.
Fine abrasions running parallel to the right outer margin with localized fiber loss.
Horizontal and slightly curved creases through the middle and lower portions of the page.
damage_notes: Major textual lacuna caused by loss of bottom right corner.
Extensive material loss and irregularity along the full top edge.
General perimeter wear, corner rounding, and edge abrasion from use and binding.
Parchment thinning and increased translucency in areas of heaviest staining.
summary: Folio 13 displays moderate to severe physical degradation typical of a heavily used 13th-century Icelandic manuscript. Dominant features are major parchment loss at the bottom right corner (with text lacuna), ragged top-edge damage, multiple water/age stains (worst on right side and top), and surface scratches/creases. No doodles, pen trials, reader marginalia, or clear scribal corrections are visible. Enlarged initials for new sections are placed in the left margin as part of the original layout. Legibility is reduced primarily by stains and edge losses rather than deliberate alteration.
